screens.name: Design Profile Assets
screens.purpose: Define the repository's aesthetic profile candidates and review language after discarding the prior warm restrained profile set.
screens.primary_action: Select a product-first profile as the default design judgment profile.

ux_rules: Keep the selected profile product-first because the current repository primarily contains app and workflow surfaces.
Keep brand/editorial and visual/music-led profiles as deliberate alternatives rather than default taste leakage.
Treat public Anthropic and third-party design skills as external references only; do not imply they are installed locally.
Do not change rendered UI or runtime CSS tokens in this pass.
Preserve separate review layers: hard rules, UX heuristics, aesthetic judgment, and token binding.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Represent the design-rule-engine profile layer as a product-context decision system, not a fixed aesthetic moodboard.
interaction_risks: External skills could be over-treated as installed runtime skills instead of reference material.
A new profile could contradict unchanged runtime CSS tokens if token-binding caveats are not explicit.
Replacing the selected profile without updating rubric prompts would leave reviewers judging against stale language.
notes: No browser evidence is required because the task changes design-system documentation and JSON assets only.
Runtime token alignment is intentionally deferred.

constraints: Replace the old candidate profiles in aesthetic-profile.json rather than adding a fifth candidate beside them.
Use OpenAI frontend-skill hard-rule ideas for restraint, section focus, brand/product clarity, and anti-card-grid checks.
Use Anthropic frontend-design as an identity process reference: subject-grounded palette, type, layout, and one justified signature element.
Use Impeccable as the register model: product surfaces and brand surfaces need different defaults for type, color, density, and motion.
Use UI UX Pro Max as a structured coverage reference for accessibility, interaction, layout, typography/color, and data/chart concerns.
Do not modify src/app/globals.css in this task.
Update stale profile-specific language in design-principles.md, core-reference-stack.md, and design-review-rubric.json.
open_decisions: Whether the runtime tone variables should be replaced to match the new selected profile is left for a later visual UI task.
Whether to install any external design skill is out of scope for this repository change.

critical_judgments: The selected profile is no longer the discarded warm restrained profile.
Candidate profiles are meaningfully different by use case rather than color mood alone.
External references are credited as references only, not as installed skills.
Rubric and principle language are aligned with the new selected profile.
must_not_regress: JSON schema compatibility for aesthetic-profile.json and design-review-rubric.json.
Separation between aesthetic profile, UX heuristic, hard-rule, and token layers.
No runtime UI or CSS token changes.

visual_constraints: No rendered UI surface changes are in scope.
No browser screenshot evidence is required for this data/documentation-only profile replacement.
Future visual work must browser-verify any token or CSS changes derived from the new profile.
